title: Intensely Smoky Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A grassy and tender one-pot Egyptian dish featuring marjoram and smoke, made diabetic-friendly with careful ingredient selection, cooked on a cedar plank for added flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine lamb, marjoram, smoked paprika, garlic, salt, and black pepper, mixing well to coat the lamb evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe pot over medium-high heat, then add the lamb mixture and cook until browned on all sides.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot, bringing the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Place the cedar plank in the pot, then cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2.5 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly, then serve the lamb hot, garnished with additional marjoram if desired.
